title: Insertion and deletion together in a Heap

content.problemStatement:
Write a program to perform the following operations:


Initialize an empty array
Given an integer N, perform N queries over this array
Given a query "+ x", insert x in your integer array at the end.
Given a query "-", remove the (Single occurrence of)maximum element of the array.
After each query print the maximum element left in the array.
Note:
For the given test cases the array will never be empty.

Input Format

The first line of each test case contains one integer N, the number of queries.
There will be N more lines, each line giving one query.


Output Format

Print the maximum element left in the array, after each query.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
1. + 4
Insert 4 into the array.
Array becomes: [4]
Max element = 4 so Output: 4
2. + 6
Insert 6 into the array.
Array becomes: [4, 6]
Max element = 6 so Output: 6
3. + 6
Insert another 6 into the array.
Array becomes: [4, 6, 6]
Max element = 6 so Output: 6
4. + 5
Insert 5 into the array.
Array becomes: [4, 6, 6, 5]
Max element = 6 so Output: 6
5. -
Remove one occurrence of the maximum element, which is 6.
Array becomes: [4, 6, 5]
Max element = 6 so Output: 6
6. -
Remove one occurrence of the maximum element, which is 6.
Array becomes: [4, 5]
Max element = 5 so Output: 5
7. -
Remove one occurrence of the maximum element, which is 5.
Array becomes: [4]
Max element = 4 so Output: 4

solution.approach:
**Handling Operations**: For each query:

**Insert operation (`+ x`)**:

Add x to the max-heap.
See this for insert operation - Insertion in a Heap Practice Problem in Heaps
After the insertion, the max-heap property ensures that the maximum element is always at the top.
Print the element at the top of the heap (the current maximum).


**Remove maximum operation (`- x`)**:

Remove the root element (maximum element) from the heap.
See this for delete operation - Deletion in a Heap Practice Problem in Heaps
After the removal, the max-heap property is restored, and the next largest element becomes the new root.
Print the new maximum element (or `-1` if the heap is empty).




**Edge Case Handling**: If the heap is empty when attempting to remove the maximum, we should output `-1` to indicate the array has no elements left.
solution.timeComplexity: Each insertion involves adding the new element at the end of the heap and then restoring the max-heap property by moving it up. In the worst case, O(log N) time. Removing elements also require O(log N) time. So for N queries total time complexity will be O(N log N).
solution.spaceComplexity: The heap vector in the MaxHeap class stores all the elements in the heap. In the worst case, if we perform N insert operations, the heap vector will store N elements. It will be O(N).
